message:
fix: make process detachment platform-specific for Windows build

Extract the Setsid syscall to platform-specific files since
syscall.SysProcAttr.Setsid is only available on Unix systems.

- spawn_unix.go: Uses Setsid to detach monitor process
- spawn_other.go: No-op on Windows (monitor is primarily Unix-focused)

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -0,0 +1,13 @@
+//go:build !unix
+
+package cli
+
+import "os/exec"
+
+// setDetachedProcess is a no-op on non-Unix platforms.
+// Process detachment requires platform-specific implementation on Windows.
+func setDetachedProcess(cmd *exec.Cmd) {
+	// On Windows, we would need to use CREATE_NEW_PROCESS_GROUP and
+	// DETACHED_PROCESS flags, but the resilience monitor is primarily
+	// designed for Unix environments where tmux runs.
+}

@@ -0,0 +1,14 @@
+//go:build unix
+
+package cli
+
+import (
+	"os/exec"
+	"syscall"
+)
+
+// setDetachedProcess configures the command to run in a new session,
+// detached from the terminal so it survives when the parent exits.
+func setDetachedProcess(cmd *exec.Cmd) {
+	cmd.SysProcAttr = &syscall.SysProcAttr{Setsid: true}
+}
